extracted.brandBackground: Schooley Mitchell provides professional cost reduction consulting services to business clients.

leadership.name: Elizabeth McMillan
leadership.background: President of Schooley Mitchell in Stratford, Ontario since 2004.
leadership.whyItMatters: Provides long-term executive leadership and technical oversight.

leadership.name: Dennis Schooley
leadership.background: Franchising and Sales & Marketing Manager since 2004; Chartered Accountant.
leadership.whyItMatters: Brings financial expertise and foundational brand knowledge as founder.

cohorts.label: Full-Time Franchisees Annual Secured Revenue
cohorts.basis: Annual Secured Revenue of 79 full-time franchisees during the 12 months ending February 28, 2025.

cohorts.label: Part-Time Franchisees Annual Secured Revenue
cohorts.basis: Annual Secured Revenue of 161 part-time franchisees during the 12 months ending May 31, 2025.

item19.notes: Annual Secured Revenue means the revenue a franchisee has received or expects to receive under customer contracts signed during the Reporting Year.

lineItems.notes: Standard or builder franchise fee is $73,000.
Covers travel, lodging, and meals for training.
Computer, fax software, telephone line, scanner, and printer.

Security deposits, insurance, utilities, licenses, professional fees, inventory, and other prepaid expenses (home office)

hiddenCosts.name: Production Fees
hiddenCosts.description: Franchisees must pay 25% (standard/development) or 30% (builder) of collected revenue for analysis and report services.

hiddenCosts.name: Assumed Business Name Registration Late Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $100 per month if not registered within 30 days of opening.

hiddenCosts.name: Bank Account Late Opening Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $100 per month if bank account is not established within 30 days of opening.

operationalRisks.title: Out-of-State Dispute Resolution
operationalRisks.description: The Franchise License Agreement requires you to resolve disputes by arbitration and litigation only in Michigan.

operationalRisks.title: Minimum Royalty and Advertising Payments
operationalRisks.description: You must make minimum royalty or advertising fund payments regardless of your sales levels.

operationalRisks.title: High Turnover Rate
operationalRisks.description: During the last 3 years, 71 outlets were terminated, not renewed, reacquired, or ceased operations.

extracted.conceptRationale: Schooley Mitchell is a professional B2B cost reduction consulting business.
extracted.staffingRationale: The business can be operated as a home-based business with minimal or no initial employees.

riskReasons: 2 high-severity operational tripwires disclosed — see the tripwires section.

underwriting.assessment: $250,000 liquid covers the $80,425 mid-point build-out without financing, though that leaves little working-capital cushion. At mid-cohort revenue, modeled cash flow after debt service is about $17,740/mo before payroll and maintenance — a thin margin of error.

insights.conceptRationale: Schooley Mitchell is a professional B2B cost reduction consulting business.

benchmark.label: General franchise (uncategorized)
benchmark.dominantRisk: Cost of goods and labor are not disclosed in any FDD — model them explicitly before trusting any margin figure.
benchmark.rampNote: Assume 6–18 months to a mature run-rate.
considerations: Budget COGS and labor explicitly — neither is in the FDD, and together they usually run 45–65% of sales.
Use the Item 20 franchisee list to validate real operating costs and ramp before committing capital.

crossCheck.message: No operating-EBITDA margin is disclosed in Item 19. The 8–20% band below is an industry estimate for this concept — validate it against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20) before relying on any margin.

insights.staffingNote: Flagged as an lightly staffed model from the FDD, so labor is adjusted down to 10–21% — the 20–35% category band assumes a staffed venue. Confirm actual staffing with Item 20 franchisees.

buildup.label: − Labor
buildup.note: lightly staffed · ≈ 0.5–1.1 FTE at ~$20/hr fully loaded

assumptions.detail: Classified as “General franchise (uncategorized)” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).
Read as lightly staffed from the FDD, which lowers the labor band applied below.
Taken from the FDD (~$750/mo); already netted in margin after fees & rent.
Estimated at the 15–35% category band — COGS is never disclosed in an FDD.
Estimated at the 10–21% lightly staffed band — labor is never disclosed in an FDD.
Utilities, insurance, and R&M estimated at 6–10% (category catch-all).
Our calculation — the disclosed top line minus the benchmarked costs above. Not a figure stated in the FDD.

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: The franchisor's statements don't show the distress signals we screen for — positive net worth and a clean audit. Still worth a quick confirmation against current figures.
body: Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
aggravators: elevated unit closures (21 in the last year, about 8% of the system)
mitigants: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Derived from the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
